image_prompt: SVG: Distance / km (0–10) vs Time of day (08:50–09:25), V-shaped line of constant-speed segments falling from 9 km at 08:50 to a minimum 1.5 km at 09:15 then rising; points every 5 min, reading 6.0 km at 09:00 and 3.0 km at 09:10 (gives 18 km h⁻¹).

render_data.title: Bounce height vs internal air pressure
render_data.prompt: The data points are: (30 kPa, 0.62 m), (35 kPa, 0.66 m), (40 kPa, 0.70 m), (45 kPa, 0.73 m), (50 kPa, 0.76 m). Imagine a straight line of best fit through these points. Using your line of best fit, select the best estimate of the bounce height at a pressure of 45 kPa.

topic: Bounce height vs drop height scatter

render_data.title: Label the axes and place your calculated point
render_data.prompt: The mean-data graph plots bounce height against drop height. Fill each blank from the palette: state the correct label for each axis, and place the data point you calculated in part (b) for the 2.0 m drop height.

slots.label: Calculated point at 2.0 m drop height

topic: CO2 and global temperature over time
image_prompt: SVG: dual-axis line graph, Concentration of CO₂ / ppm (250–390, left) and Temperature / °C (13.5–14.5, right) vs Year (1000–2000); both flat until ~1800 then spike sharply (positive correlation); key for the two curves.

Night scene with an erupting volcano (lava, ash plume), concentric sound wavefronts emanating from the crater, distant observer with stopwatch. Labels: 'Light: instantaneous', 'Sound: travels at 330 m s⁻¹'.

topic: Van de Graaff discharge (400 000 V, 16 000 A)
image_prompt: Van de Graaff generator (column, charged belt slot, large positive dome) discharging via a spark to an earthed sphere. Labels: dimension line marking 400 000 V between dome and earth, current arrow marked 16 000 A along the discharge.

Charged operator's hand near a PCB inside an electronic device chassis on a workbench. Coiled grounding strap clipped from the chassis to an earthed point. ESD discharge spark from hand to clip. Labels: Charged operator, PCB, Grounding strap, to earth.

render_data.title: Identify the particle released in each decay
render_data.prompt: For each decay equation, place it in the bin for the particle it releases. Alpha: mass −4, atomic number −2. Beta: mass unchanged, atomic number +1.

image_prompt: Three-column table: Isotope | Half-life / years | Additional information. Rows: Curium-242 (4 730 yr, artificial), Carbon-14 (5 700 yr, organic), Potassium-40 (1.3×10⁹ yr, volcanic rocks), Uranium-238 (4.5×10⁹ yr, ore).

render_data.title: Classify the variables in the volleyball bounce experiment
render_data.prompt: Place each variable into the correct bin: the one you change (independent), the one you measure (dependent), or the ones you keep constant (control).

image_prompt: Pressure gauge dial 0–120 kPa (outer black) with inner psi scale 0–18 (red), needle pointing to 95 kPa (between 80 and 100, close to 100). Volleyball on a sports-court background. Stem connecting gauge to ball. Labels: 'Pressure gauge', 'Volleyball'.

topic: Rugby ball + tennis ball drop animation scene
image_prompt: Indoor scene: man on a ladder holding a rugby ball at 2.0 m, woman with a ruler at the wall. Control panel on right with Rugby ball / Tennis ball headers and three drop heights (2.0/1.5/1.0 m) each with 'Drop ball' buttons; selection arrow at 2.0 m rugby.

topic: Trial data: 2.0 m drop, three repetitions in mixed units
image_prompt: Notebook on a wooden desk: 'Rugby ball bounce experiment'; 2.0 m drop labelled; trial table with Trial 1 0.64 m, Trial 2 58 cm, Trial 3 6.7 × 10² mm. Marker pens beside.
Lined-paper notes 'My data' with five unsorted drop/bounce pairs in mixed units: 3.0 m → 0.84 m, 3.5 m → 92 cm, 2.5 m → 0.70 m, 150 cm → 42 cm, 1×10² cm → 24 cm. Small bar+arc icons for each pair.
Two players on an outdoor tennis court with net; tennis ball in flight; racquets visible.

topic: Tennis ball in warm water bath apparatus
image_prompt: Beaker of warm water on an electronic heater with a thermometer dipped in; tennis ball submerged inside. Labels: Warm water, Tennis ball, Thermometer, Electronic heater.
Coloured bubbles for transport options (Tram 30 min, Walking 70 min, Bicycle 20 min, Car 10 min, Bus 15 min) with a directional arrow from Kieran to a School icon; banner 'Distance: 6 km'.
